provenance.source_corpus: Recreating a Functioning Forest Soil in Reclaimed Oil Sands in Northern Alberta: An Approach for Measuring Success in Ecological Restoration

source_spans.text: On the reclamation treatments, availability of nitrate, calcium, magnesium, and sulfur were generally higher than in the natural forest ecotypes

nodes.name: Availability of nitrate, calcium, magnesium, and sulfur

source_spans.text: On the reclamation treatments, availability of nitrate, calcium, magnesium, and sulfur were generally higher than in the natural forest ecotypes

nodes.name: Availability of ammonium, P, K, and Mn

source_spans.text: while ammonium, P , K, and Mn were generally lower
Reclamation treatments tended to have more bare ground, grasses, and forbs

nodes.name: Moss, lichen, shrubs, trees, or woody debris

source_spans.text: but less moss, lichen, shrubs, trees, or woody debris than natural forests
Rates of litter decomposition were lower on all reclamation treatments
Development of an organic layer appeared to be facilitated by the presence of shrubs
Development of an organic layer appeared to be facilitated by the presence of shrubs
With repeated applications of fertilizers, measured variables for the peat-mineral amendments fell within the range of natural variability at about 20 yr
With repeated applications of fertilizers, measured variables for the peat-mineral amendments fell within the range of natural variability at about 20 yr
An intermediate subsoil layer reduced the need for fertilizer
An intermediate subsoil layer reduced the need for fertilizer
conditions resembling natural forests were reached about 15 yr after a single fertilizer application
if tailings sand, which has a higher hydrocarbon content than overburden, is reclaimed with a subsoil cap, the tailings sand may reduce that subsoil’s inherent microbial activity

the tailings sand may reduce that subsoil’s inherent microbial activity
but mycorrhizae can be restored using peat additions
but mycorrhizae can be restored using peat additions
The only variable of significance (P = 0.0021) was soil C/N ratio
Inclusion of groundcover data into the model for FH development on reclamation treatments indicated that shrub cover was a contributing factor

when understorey plants disappeared
